questions.stem: A machine costing ₹5,00,000 (residual value ₹50,000, life 10 years) is depreciated on SLM. After 4 years, remaining life is revised to 3 years with the same residual value. Depreciation for year 5 = ?

options.explanation: The original SLM charge — trap: ignores the revision.
Planted-wrong key: fluent prose claims this, but WDV after 4 yrs = 5,00,000 − 4×45,000 = 3,20,000; (3,20,000 − 50,000)/3 = 90,000.
Divides WDV by 3 but forgets to remove residual value first: 3,20,000/3 ≈ 1,06,667 rounded wrong — no basis.

questions.source: fixture — deliberately wrong key; the runner MUST flag this

questions.stem: Asset cost ₹2,40,000, residual value ₹40,000, useful life 8 years. Annual SLM depreciation = ?

options.explanation: Adds residual value instead of subtracting.

questions.source: fixture — correct key; the runner must pass this
